engineGuide.h2: Volkswagen Transporter Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Volkswagen Transporter across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: Entry-level 84PS EA188 unit; widely used in Panel Van fleets; timing chain tensioner concerns defined replacement demand.
compatibleVariants: Panel Van Startline, Panel Van Trendline, Kombi Startline, Chassis Cab
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner failure (60k-90k miles)
EGR valve clogging on short-journey use
DPF regeneration issues in urban operation
entries.cta: Get quotes for CAAA Volkswagen Transporter engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: Mid-output 102PS EA188; the Transporter's volume seller across Panel Van, Kombi and passenger variants.
compatibleVariants: Panel Van Trendline, Panel Van Highline, Kombi, Shuttle, Caravelle SE
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear (EA188 generation)
EGR cooler leaks on high-mileage examples
Turbo actuator sticking above 100k miles
entries.cta: Get quotes for CAAB Volkswagen Transporter engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: High-output 140PS BiTDI twin-turbo; sought-after for heavy payloads and camper conversions; complex twin-turbo system.
compatibleVariants: Crew Cab Highline, Caravelle Executive, Camper High-Output, Highline 4MOTION
commonFailures: Secondary turbo actuator failure (80k+ miles)
BiTDI intercooler pipe leaks under load
DPF/AdBlue system faults on Euro 6 variants
entries.cta: Get quotes for CFCA Volkswagen Transporter engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: Euro 6 102PS EA288 update; introduced AdBlue/SCR; common in T6 Panel Van and commercial fleets.
compatibleVariants: Panel Van Euro 6, Kombi Euro 6, Chassis Cab Euro 6, Shuttle Euro 6
commonFailures: AdBlue injector crystallisation faults
NOx sensor failures triggering limp mode
EGR cooler leaks on high-mileage commercial use
entries.cta: Get quotes for CXFA Volkswagen Transporter engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: Latest T6.1 110PS EA288 evo; refined emissions systems; dominant in 2019-2024 registrations.
compatibleVariants: Panel Van T6.1, Kombi T6.1, Shuttle T6.1, Caravelle T6.1
commonFailures: AdBlue dosing pump faults (early T6.1)
DPF pressure sensor errors on stop-start use
Turbo wastegate sticking above 90k miles
entries.cta: Get quotes for DSHA Volkswagen Transporter engine replacement

entries.title: 2.5L Diesel
entries.history: Entry 131PS five-cylinder TDI; robust bottom end; injector pump wear defined later-life replacement patterns.
compatibleVariants: Panel Van, Kombi, Crew Cab, Chassis Cab (pre-facelift T5)
commonFailures: VE injector pump wear (rattling noise, hard starting)
Turbo oil seal leaks above 120k miles
EGR valve carbon buildup on urban use
entries.cta: Get quotes for AXB Volkswagen Transporter engine replacement

entries.title: 2.5L Diesel
entries.history: High-output 174PS five-cylinder; sought for heavy-duty commercial and camper use; specialist rebuild demand.
compatibleVariants: Panel Van Highline, Kombi Highline, Crew Cab Highline, Camper Conversions
commonFailures: Injector pump high-pressure failure (costly)
Turbocharger bearing wear under sustained load
Cylinder head gasket failure on overheated examples
entries.cta: Get quotes for AXD Volkswagen Transporter engine replacement

entries.title: 3.2L Petrol
entries.history: Smooth 235PS VR6; rare in commercial use; enthusiast and camper conversion favourite; specialist rebuild only.
compatibleVariants: Caravelle Executive, Caravelle Highline, Camper Premium Conversions
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear (100k+ miles)
Oil consumption from valve stem seals
Coolant pipe leaks on narrow-angle VR6 layout

commonProblems.h2: Volkswagen Transporter Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Volkswagen Transporter's 2.0 TDI EA188/EA288 and legacy 2.5 TDI engines dominate UK replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use this decision tool to compare repair vs replacement costs for your specific variant.

problems.group: Volkswagen Transporter Timing Chain Tensioner Failure
problems.h4: Volkswagen Transporter Timing Chain Tensioner Failure - Why EA188 Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Volkswagen Transporter Panel Van, Kombi, Crew Cab 2.0 TDI (2010-2015, CAAA/CAAB/CFCA EA188)
problems.rootCause: Early EA188 tensioner design allowed chain slack on cold starts, leading to rattling noise and potential chain skip or jump.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace tensioner, chain guide, inspect chain; oil/fluid change
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early before chain stretch; prevents catastrophic failure

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded tensioner, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage commercial vehicles; eliminates repeat failure risk

problems.recommendation: On a Transporter worth £8,000-£15,000 with over 100k miles, a rebuilt engine with upgraded tensioner offers better long-term value than preventative chain work. For lower-mileage examples under 70k miles, preventative replacement may extend life cost-effectively.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt EA188 engine prices for your Volkswagen Transporter

problems.h4: Volkswagen Transporter EGR Cooler Failure - Why 2.0 TDI Engines Leak Coolant
problems.affectedModels: Volkswagen Transporter Panel Van, Kombi, Shuttle 2.0 TDI (2010-2019, CAAB/CXFA/DCXA)
problems.rootCause: EGR cooler internal cracks allow coolant to mix with exhaust gases, triggering warning lights, coolant loss, and potential head gasket stress.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove old cooler, fit new/reconditioned unit, reset fault codes, coolant refill
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if engine otherwise healthy; does not address underlying carbon buildup

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with updated EGR components, full emissions system check, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for high-mileage commercial use where downtime costs outweigh repair savings

problems.recommendation: If your Transporter is used for short urban journeys (high EGR load), replacing the cooler alone may recur. For fleet vehicles or high-mileage examples, a rebuilt engine with updated emissions components offers better uptime and warranty protection.
problems.cta: Get quotes for CAAB/CXFA Volkswagen Transporter engine replacement

problems.h4: Volkswagen Transporter BiTDI Twin-Turbo Failure - Why CFCA Engines Lose Boost
problems.affectedModels: Volkswagen Transporter Crew Cab Highline, Caravelle Executive, Camper 2.0 BiTDI (2012-2019, CFCA)
problems.rootCause: Secondary turbo actuator sticking or vacuum hose leaks cause uneven boost, limp mode, and eventual turbo bearing wear under sustained load.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace faulty actuator, vacuum lines, intercooler pipes; reset adaptation values
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if primary turbo healthy; does not address wear on secondary turbo bearings

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt BiTDI unit with upgraded turbo components, full system calibration, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for camper conversions or heavy-duty commercial use where reliability is critical

problems.recommendation: BiTDI engines are complex; if one turbo has failed, the other is often near end-of-life. For Transporters used for towing or camper conversions, a rebuilt BiTDI engine with upgraded turbos offers peace of mind and warranty coverage.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt CFCA BiTDI engine prices for your Volkswagen Transporter

problems.group: Volkswagen Transporter 2.5 TDI Injector Pump Failure
problems.h4: Volkswagen Transporter 2.5 TDI Injector Pump Failure - Why AXB/AXD Engines Rattle
problems.affectedModels: Volkswagen Transporter Panel Van, Kombi, Crew Cab 2.5 TDI (2003-2009, AXB/AXD)
problems.rootCause: VE-style injector pump internal wear causes rattling noise on cold start, hard starting, and eventual loss of fuel pressure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove old pump, fit reconditioned/new unit, code injectors, bleed fuel system
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if engine block/head healthy; does not address turbo or injector wear

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 2.5 TDI with upgraded pump, injectors, turbo check, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for high-mileage commercial vehicles where total cost of ownership matters

problems.recommendation: The 2.5 TDI is robust but pump failure is costly. If your Transporter has other age-related wear (turbo, injectors), a rebuilt engine with upgraded pump offers better value than pump-only repair.
problems.cta: Get quotes for AXB/AXD Volkswagen Transporter engine replacement

problems.h4: Volkswagen Transporter DPF/AdBlue System Faults - Why Euro 6 Engines Enter Limp Mode
problems.affectedModels: Volkswagen Transporter Panel Van, Shuttle, Caravelle Euro 6 2.0 TDI (2015-2024, CXFA/DSHA/DFHA)
problems.rootCause: DPF ash buildup, AdBlue injector crystallisation, or NOx sensor faults trigger emissions warnings and forced limp mode to protect the system.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: DPF off-car clean or replace AdBlue injector/NOx sensor, reset adaptations, road test
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable for vehicles with otherwise healthy engines and regular motorway use

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt Euro 6 unit with updated emissions components, full system calibration, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage commercial fleets where uptime and warranty coverage are priorities

problems.recommendation: If your Transporter does mostly short urban trips, DPF/AdBlue issues will recur. For commercial users, a rebuilt Euro 6 engine with updated emissions components and warranty offers better long-term value and reduced downtime.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt Euro 6 Volkswagen Transporter engine prices

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Volkswagen Transporter
finalCta.paragraph: Timing chain, EGR, turbo, injector pump and emissions faults are the most common reasons UK Transporter owners seek engine replacement. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Volkswagen Transporter specialists who supply rebuilt, reconditioned and used engines with upgraded components addressing the original failure point. Every engine includes a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery or supply & fit options. Compare prices today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Volkswagen Transporter Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Volkswagen Transporter and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Volkswagen Transporter Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Volkswagen Transporter is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement.

items.title: Volkswagen Transporter 1.9L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.9 TDI powered early T4 and entry T5 Transporters; a simple, robust unit favoured by budget-conscious commercial users, now largely replaced by 2.0 TDI in later generations.
commonFailurePoints: injector pump wear on high-mileage examples
items.cta: Get quotes for Volkswagen Transporter 1.9L diesel engines

items.title: Volkswagen Transporter 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The backbone of the Transporter range from T5 facelift through T6.1; EA188 and EA288 families cover 84-140hp outputs across Panel Van, Kombi, Crew Cab and passenger variants.
commonFailurePoints: EA188 timing chain tensioner wear; EA288 AdBlue/DPF system faults
items.cta: Get quotes for Volkswagen Transporter 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Volkswagen Transporter 2.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: The five-cylinder 2.5 TDI defined the early T5 generation; sought for its torque and character in camper conversions, though injector pump and turbo wear drive replacement enquiries.
commonFailurePoints: VE injector pump rattling; turbo bearing wear under sustained load
items.cta: Get quotes for Volkswagen Transporter 2.5L diesel engines

items.title: Volkswagen Transporter 2.8L VR6 Engines
items.description: The narrow-angle VR6 offered smooth power in early T5 Caravelle and camper conversions; rare in commercial use, specialist rebuilders handle these premium replacements.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain tensioner wear; coolant pipe leaks on VR6 layout
items.cta: Compare Volkswagen Transporter 2.8L petrol engine prices

items.title: Volkswagen Transporter 3.2L V6 Engines
items.description: The flagship 3.2 V6 powered high-spec Caravelle and enthusiast camper builds; low volume but high-value replacements requiring certified specialist rebuilders. fuelTypes.h2: Volkswagen Transporter Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide

EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Volkswagen Transporter specialists who supply rebuilt, reconditioned and used engines with upgraded components addressing the original failure point. Every engine includes a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery or supply & fit options. Compare prices today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.

engineYears.h2: Volkswagen Transporter Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers all major Volkswagen Transporter engine changes from T4 launch to T6.1 end-of-production, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The T5 launch introduced the robust 2.5 TDI five-cylinder alongside rare VR6/V6 petrol options for Caravelle and camper conversions.
keyChanges: T5 platform replaced T4 with front-wheel drive layout
2.5 TDI AXB/AXD became the volume commercial engine
2.8 VR6 and 3.2 V6 offered in Caravelle passenger variants
Panel Van, Kombi, Crew Cab, Chassis Cab body styles established
knownFor: 2.5 TDI injector pump rattling defines later-life replacements
Robust bottom end but turbo wear under sustained commercial load
VR6/V6 petrol sought by camper enthusiasts despite rarity
Early T5 commercial fleets now reaching high-mileage replacement phase
years.cta: Get quotes for 2003-2009 Volkswagen Transporter replacement engines
years.ticker: Volkswagen Transporter 2.5 TDI injector pump • Volkswagen Transporter 3.2 V6 timing chain • Volkswagen Transporter T5 engine replacement cost
enquiries: Volkswagen Transporter T5 engine replacement cost

years.description: The 2010 facelift introduced the EA188 2.0 TDI family, replacing the 2.5 TDI as the volume engine and bringing timing chain tensioner concerns to UK replacement enquiries.
keyChanges: EA188 2.0 TDI replaced 2.5 TDI as the volume commercial engine
CAAA (84PS), CAAB (102PS), CFCA (140PS BiTDI) outputs introduced
DPF fitted across range; Euro 5 compliance mandatory
Start/Stop technology introduced on later facelift models
popularModels: Volkswagen Transporter Panel Van 2.0 TDI CAAA
Volkswagen Transporter Crew Cab 2.0 BiTDI CFCA
knownFor: EA188 timing chain tensioner failure 60k-90k miles dominates enquiries
BiTDI CFCA twin-turbo complexity drives specialist rebuild demand
DPF regeneration issues on urban-stop-start commercial use
Highest UK replacement volume generation for Panel Van fleets
years.cta: Get quotes for 2010-2015 Volkswagen Transporter replacement engines
years.ticker: Volkswagen Transporter EA188 timing chain • Volkswagen Transporter CFCA BiTDI turbo • Volkswagen Transporter 2.0 TDI DPF fault

years.description: The T6 introduced EA288 Euro 6 engines with AdBlue/SCR systems, bringing new emissions-related failure patterns to UK replacement enquiries.
keyChanges: EA288 2.0 TDI replaced EA188 with Euro 6 compliance
AdBlue/SCR systems fitted; CXFA, DCXA, DCXB codes introduced
Updated interior, infotainment and driver aids across range
Panel Van, Kombi, Shuttle, Caravelle variants refreshed
popularModels: Volkswagen Transporter Panel Van Euro 6 CXFA
knownFor: AdBlue injector crystallisation and NOx sensor faults common
DPF pressure sensor errors on urban commercial use
Euro 6 complexity drives higher specialist rebuild pricing
Strong demand for ULEZ-compliant replacements in London/metro areas
years.cta: Get quotes for 2015-2019 Volkswagen Transporter replacement engines
years.ticker: Volkswagen Transporter Euro 6 AdBlue fault • Volkswagen Transporter CXFA DPF warning • Volkswagen Transporter T6 engine replacement ULEZ
enquiries: Volkswagen Transporter T6 engine replacement ULEZ

years.preview: T6.1 Final Generation: EA288 evo Refinement
years.description: The T6.1 refined the EA288 platform with DSHA/DFHA 110PS outputs, representing the final Transporter generation before the all-electric ID. Buzz transition.
keyChanges: DSHA/DFHA 110PS EA288 evo became the standard output
Updated LED lighting, digital cockpit and driver assistance
Final Transporter generation before ID. Buzz electric successor
popularModels: Volkswagen Transporter T6.1 Panel Van DSHA
knownFor: AdBlue dosing pump faults on early T6.1 examples
Refined EA288 evo offers improved reliability over earlier EA288
Strong residual values drive rebuilt engine demand for late-model vans
Final generation ensures parts availability for foreseeable future
years.cta: Get quotes for 2019-2024 Volkswagen Transporter replacement engines
years.ticker: Volkswagen Transporter T6.1 AdBlue pump • Volkswagen Transporter DSHA engine replacement • Volkswagen Transporter latest model engine cost
enquiries: Volkswagen Transporter latest model engine cost

faq.h2: Volkswagen Transporter Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Volkswagen Transporter engine replacement cost UK?
items.answer: Volkswagen Transporter engine replacement costs typically range from £895 for a used 1.9 TDI to £4,250 for a rebuilt 2.0 BiTDI, depending on engine code, variant and condition type. Supply-only prices exclude fitting labour.
keyPoints: Used engines: £895-£2,450 depending on mileage and grade
Rebuilt 2.0 TDI EA188/EA288: £1,095-£3,850 typical range
BiTDI CFCA and V6 petrol command premium pricing
Supply & fit adds £425-£850 to engine price
items.cta: Compare live Volkswagen Transporter engine prices now

items.question: What engine codes fit my Volkswagen Transporter?
items.answer: Your Volkswagen Transporter's exact engine code depends on variant, year and emissions standard - common codes include CAAA, CAAB, CFCA for 2.0 TDI and AXB, AXD for 2.5 TDI. Use our registration checker for instant verification.
keyPoints: EA188 codes: CAAA (84PS), CAAB (102PS), CFCA (140PS BiTDI)
EA288 codes: CXFA, DCXA, DSHA (Euro 6 variants)
Legacy codes: AXB/AXD (2.5 TDI), AXL (3.2 V6)
DVLA lookup ensures 100% compatibility before ordering
items.cta: Check your Volkswagen Transporter engine code instantly

items.question: Is the Volkswagen Transporter 2.0 TDI EA188 timing chain failure common?
items.answer: Yes, early EA188 2.0 TDI engines (CAAA/CAAB/CFCA 2010-2015) are prone to timing chain tensioner failure between 60k-90k miles, causing rattling on cold start and potential chain skip.
keyPoints: Failure typically presents as rattling noise on cold start
Ignoring symptoms risks chain jump and catastrophic engine damage
Rebuilt engines include upgraded tensioner addressing the root cause
Preventative replacement viable under 70k miles on otherwise healthy engines
items.cta: Get quotes for EA188 Volkswagen Transporter engine replacement

items.question: Should I repair or replace my Volkswagen Transporter engine?
items.answer: For high-mileage commercial Volkswagen Transporters over 100k miles, replacement with a rebuilt engine usually offers better long-term value than major repairs, especially with warranty coverage and reduced downtime risk.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engines include upgraded failure-point components
12-month warranty protects against repeat failure costs
Supply & fit options minimise commercial vehicle downtime
Repair may suit lower-mileage examples under 70k miles
items.cta: Compare repair vs replacement costs for your Volkswagen Transporter

items.question: Are used Volkswagen Transporter engines reliable?
items.answer: Grade A used Volkswagen Transporter engines from reputable breakers can be reliable if compression-tested and sourced from low-mileage donors, but carry higher uncertainty than rebuilt units with warranty.
keyPoints: Always request compression test report before purchase
Grade A/B/C clarity on donor vehicle mileage and condition
Used engines typically carry shorter warranty (3-6 months)
Best suited to budget replacements on older T4/T5 models
items.cta: Check used Volkswagen Transporter engine availability today

items.question: Does a rebuilt Volkswagen Transporter engine come with a warranty?
items.answer: Yes, every rebuilt Volkswagen Transporter engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK specialists, covering parts and labour for qualifying failures.
keyPoints: Warranty terms vary slightly by supplier; confirm before ordering
Coverage typically excludes misuse, poor maintenance or incorrect fitting
Supply & fit installations often include extended labour coverage
Warranty documentation provided with every rebuilt unit
items.cta: Get rebuilt Volkswagen Transporter engine quotes with warranty

items.question: Can I get supply and fit for my Volkswagen Transporter engine replacement?
items.answer: Yes, EngineMarket's UK network includes Volkswagen Transporter specialists offering supply & fit services nationwide, with courtesy van options to minimise commercial downtime.
keyPoints: Fitting cost typically adds £425-£850 to engine price
4MOTION and BiTDI calibration expertise available at specialist centres
Courtesy van options subject to availability and location
Book fitting concurrently with engine order to reduce downtime
items.cta: Find Volkswagen Transporter engine supply & fit deals near me

items.question: Will a replacement Volkswagen Transporter engine pass MOT?
items.answer: Yes, rebuilt and reconditioned Volkswagen Transporter engines supplied through EngineMarket are emissions-compliant and MOT-ready when correctly installed and calibrated by a qualified technician.
keyPoints: Euro 5/6 engines retain original emissions equipment (DPF/AdBlue)
ECU coding and adaptation reset required post-installation
Specialist fitters provide calibration documentation for MOT tester
Always confirm emissions compliance with your chosen supplier
items.cta: Compare MOT-compliant Volkswagen Transporter engine options

items.question: How long does Volkswagen Transporter engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only Volkswagen Transporter engines typically dispatch within 1-3 working days; supply & fit installations require 1-2 days workshop time depending on variant complexity and 4MOTION calibration needs.
keyPoints: Next-day delivery available on common codes (CAAA, CAAB, CXFA)
BiTDI, V6 and 4MOTION variants may require longer lead times
Supply & fit bookings subject to specialist workshop availability
Plan for 2-3 days total downtime for commercial fleet vehicles
items.cta: Get quotes with delivery timeframe for your Volkswagen Transporter

items.question: Which Volkswagen Transporter engine is most reliable?
items.answer: The later EA288 2.0 TDI (CXFA/DSHA Euro 6) and the robust 2.5 TDI five-cylinder (AXB/AXD) are generally considered the most reliable Volkswagen Transporter engines when properly maintained, though all benefit from rebuilt upgrades addressing known failure points.
keyPoints: EA288 Euro 6 refined EA188 timing chain concerns
2.5 TDI robust bottom end but injector pump wear at high mileage
Rebuilt units with upgraded components offer best long-term reliability
Regular servicing and motorway runs extend any engine's lifespan
items.cta: Compare reliable rebuilt Volkswagen Transporter engine options
